name: La Rioja (Argentina)

colors.symbolism: the colours of the Argentine national flag
the colours of the Argentine national flag
the federalist red of Facundo Quiroga and the Federal League

description: Horizontally divided white over light blue, with a red diagonal stripe running from the upper fly to the lower hoist; the provincial coat of arms is set in the centre.
history: Adopted by provincial law 7148 on 24 November 2001 after a public competition won by educator Marta Estela Quintero. The design recalls the bandera tricolor cruzada of the Federal League — the federalist confederation of which La Rioja was a member during the early-19th-century civil wars — and honours the Riojan caudillo Juan Facundo Quiroga (1788–1835).

features.symbolism: the celestial blue and white of the Argentine national flag

features.role: red diagonal stripe of the Federal League
features.symbolism: the federalist cause led by Riojan caudillo Juan Facundo Quiroga; also recalls the Bandera de Artigas of the Liga Federal

features.role: coat of arms of La Rioja Province
features.symbolism: the provincial coat of arms — a Sun of May above clasped hands holding a pike topped with a Phrygian cap, set within a wreath of laurel — recalling liberty, the May Revolution and the federalist heritage of the province
